🪢 Tether User Guide

Getting Started

Overview Installation Quick Start

Features

tmux Session Mirroring Fresh PTY Terminals Web Interface

Usage

CLI Options Phone Access tmux Tips

Advanced

Architecture Security Troubleshooting Development

Overview

Tether is a mobile web terminal controller that lets you access and control your laptop's terminal sessions from your phone over your local network. It works in two modes:

  • tmux Session Mirroring — Auto-discovers your existing tmux sessions and shows them as panels in the web app. Input and output sync bidirectionally between your laptop and phone.
  • Fresh PTY Terminals — Spawns brand new bash sessions with full terminal emulation when tmux isn't available.
┌─────────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────┐ │ Your Laptop │ │ Your Phone │ │ │ │ │ │ tmux session: work │ ◄──────► │ Panel: bash (%0) │ │ ┌─────────────────┐ │ WiFi/WS │ ┌───────────────┐ │ │ │ $ ls │ │ ◄────────► │ │ $ ls │ │ │ │ file1 file2 │ │ │ │ file1 file2 │ │ │ │ $ _ │ │ │ │ $ _ │ │ │ └─────────────────┘ │ │ └───────────────┘ │ └─────────────────────┘ └─────────────────────┘

Installation

Prerequisites

  • Rust 1.75+ — Install via rustup
  • tmux (recommended) — For session mirroring
  • Linux or macOS — PTY support via POSIX APIs

Install tmux

# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo apt install tmux

# macOS
brew install tmux

# Fedora
sudo dnf install tmux

Build from Source

git clone https://github.com/ssnelavala-masstcs/tether.git
cd tether
cargo build --release

# Binary is at: target/release/tether

Quick Start

1

Start tmux sessions

tmux new -s work    # Create a session named "work"
# Press Ctrl+B, then D to detach

tmux new -s monitor # Create another session
2

Start Tether

./target/release/tether serve --password "yourpassword" --allow-lan

You'll see output like:

INFO Starting Tether server on http://0.0.0.0:8080
INFO Discovered 3 terminal tabs
INFO   mirror-0 -> /dev/pts/10 (PID 1234, cmd: bash)
INFO Access Tether at: http://192.168.1.100:8080
3

Open on your phone

Open the URL shown in the logs (e.g., http://192.168.1.100:8080) on your phone's browser.

4

Login and use

Enter your password. Your tmux sessions appear as tabs — tap to switch between them. Type commands just like a real terminal.

tmux Session Mirroring

When you start Tether, it automatically discovers tmux sessions and creates mirror panels for each pane.

How it works

DirectionMechanism
Laptop → Phone (output)tmux capture-pane -p -e polled every 200ms
Phone → Laptop (input)tmux send-keys for each keystroke

What gets discovered

  • All tmux sessions and their panes
  • Each pane becomes a separate tab in the web app
  • Non-tmux bash sessions are also discovered (but require manual setup for mirroring)
Tip: Name your tmux sessions meaningfully (e.g., tmux new -s server, tmux new -s logs) so they're easy to identify in the web app.

Fresh PTY Terminals

If no tmux sessions are found, or when you tap "+ New", Tether creates a brand new bash session using raw forkpty.

  • Full PTY with proper terminal emulation (colors, cursor movement, etc.)
  • Independent from your laptop's existing terminals
  • Supports resize events, scrollback, and all xterm.js features
Note: Fresh PTY terminals are not connected to your laptop's existing sessions. They're standalone bash sessions created specifically for the web app.

Web Interface

Dashboard Layout

  • Header — App title, "+ New" button, "↻" refresh button
  • Tabs bar — One tab per terminal/session, with connection status indicator
  • Terminal area — Full-screen xterm.js terminal for the active tab

Tab Controls

ElementAction
Tab clickSwitch to that terminal
✕ on tabClose that terminal (disconnects WebSocket)
Green dotConnected and streaming
Red dotDisconnected (will auto-reconnect)

Terminal Features

  • Full keyboard input (letters, numbers, special characters, arrows)
  • Copy/paste via phone clipboard
  • Auto-resize on orientation change
  • 10,000 line scrollback buffer
  • Gruvbox-inspired color theme

CLI Options

Usage: tether serve [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -p, --password <PASSWORD>  Password for authentication
  -P, --port <PORT>          Port to bind to [default: 8080]
      --allow-lan            Allow LAN access (binds to 0.0.0.0)
  -h, --help                 Print help

Examples

# Localhost only (default)
./target/release/tether serve --password "secret"

# LAN access on custom port
./target/release/tether serve --password "secret" --allow-lan --port 3000

Phone Access

Method 1: Direct IP

  1. Start Tether with --allow-lan
  2. Note the IP address in the server logs
  3. Open that URL on your phone's browser

Method 2: QR Code

Tether prints a QR code in the server logs when started with --allow-lan. Scan it with your phone's camera to open the URL instantly.

Method 3: ngrok (remote access)

# In one terminal, start tether
./target/release/tether serve --password "secret" --port 8080

# In another terminal, start ngrok
ngrok http 8080

# Open the ngrok URL on your phone from anywhere
Warning: When using ngrok or any public tunnel, your Tether instance is exposed to the internet. Use a strong password and consider additional security layers.

tmux Tips for Tether

  • Detach, don't kill: Press Ctrl+B, D to detach from tmux. Sessions keep running and Tether keeps mirroring them.
  • Multiple panes: Each tmux pane becomes a separate tab in Tether. Split panes with Ctrl+B, % (vertical) or Ctrl+B, " (horizontal).
  • Named sessions: Use tmux new -s name for easy identification.
  • Long-running commands: Commands like top, htop, tail -f stream output in real-time to the web app.

Architecture

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Your Laptop                                      │
│                                                    │
│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐ │
│  │ Axum HTTP  │  │ PTY Mgr    │  │ Mirror Mgr   │ │
│  │ + WS Srv   │◄►│ (forkpty)  │  │ (tmux CLI)   │ │
│  └─────┬──────┘  └────────────┘  └──────┬───────┘ │
│        │                                 │         │
│        │  WebSocket (real-time I/O)      │         │
│        ▼                                 ▼         │
└────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬────────┘
         │ LAN (WiFi)                      │
         ▼                                 ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Your Phone                                       │
│                                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  xterm.js Terminal (real-time rendering)   │   │
│  │  [Tab 1] [Tab 2] [Tab 3]  [+ New] [↻]     │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Security

LayerImplementation
Access ControlArgon2id password hashing, session cookies with 7-day expiry
Rate Limiting5 failed attempts per 10-minute window per IP
NetworkDefaults to 127.0.0.1; 0.0.0.0 only with --allow-lan
CookiesHttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, Path=/
Warning: Tether is designed for trusted LAN environments. Do not expose it to the public internet without additional security (reverse proxy, TLS, firewall rules).

Troubleshooting

No tmux sessions discovered

# Check if tmux is running
tmux list-sessions

# If not, start a session
tmux new -s work

Terminal not responding

  • Tap the ↻ refresh button to reconnect
  • Check that the tmux session is still alive
  • Restart Tether if needed

Can't connect from phone

  • Ensure both devices are on the same WiFi network
  • Check firewall settings on your laptop
  • Verify the IP address is correct
  • Try curl http://<ip>:8080 from another machine to test

Development

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/ssnelavala-masstcs/tether.git
cd tether

# Run with debug logging
RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- serve --password "dev"

# Build release
cargo build --release

# Code quality
cargo clippy -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check

Project Structure

src/
├── main.rs             # CLI entry point
├── server.rs           # Axum HTTP server + routing
├── pty_manager.rs      # forkpty: spawn/resize/kill PTYs
├── terminal_mirror.rs  # tmux discovery + mirroring
├── auth.rs             # Argon2 password + sessions
├── ws_handler.rs       # WebSocket ↔ PTY/tmux bridge
└── state.rs            # Shared app state

assets/
├── index.html          # Dashboard
├── login.html          # Login page
├── css/style.css       # Mobile-first styles
└── js/app.js           # Frontend logic
